content: We can reframe Self-Refine’s single-perspective iterative refinement framework by integrating multiple inconsistent self-reflection perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanisms, creating a multi-perspective Self-Refine framework that outperforms the original method without adding extra training or external data. This hybrid framework lets the LLM critique its own output from varied contradictory problem-solving lenses rather than a single default framing, catching more errors and blind spots that a single pass of self-feedback would miss.
